title: The Castle Is Freed
description: On pages 7–10, this part focuses on the castle is freed, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: The Castle Is Freed. Luffy defeats Wapol and destroys the symbol of his selfish rule, clearing the way for Drum’s rebirth. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: He fought for the sake of this country...!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: I'm sure drum can finally be born anew now!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: T-There's a weird creature over there!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Dumbass! That's our new crewwate I found!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: But he ran away because you scared him off!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Feelin' happy, pappies? Why don't you bring over any wounded to the patient rooms inside.

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: And by that, I mean every- one.

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: It's better for you to recover fully before setting off, you know.

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Be quiet! If we don't escape now, we'll be another 2 whole days behind in our plan to arrive in Alabasta!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: You also don't want to see VIVI suffer any more than she has already, right!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: I said get back to the patient rooms!

summary: This part highlights the castle is freed and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
